agent.name: Machine Walkaround
agent.description: AI-powered machine walkaround inspection assistant for Vermeer Manufacturing. Uses Azure OpenAI Vision to analyze equipment photos, identify components, detect wear/damage, and guide pre-operation inspections.
agent.author: RAPP Pipeline

metadata.name: MachineWalkaround
metadata.description: AI-powered machine walkaround inspection assistant for Vermeer equipment. Analyzes photos using Azure OpenAI Vision to identify components, detect wear/damage, guide inspections, and answer equipment questions. Supports trenchers, brush chippers, stump cutters, and horizontal directional drills.

question.description: User question about the image or machine

actions.name: analyze_image
actions.description: General-purpose image analysis — send a photo of any machine component and get AI analysis

input.question: What component is this and does it show any wear?

example.output:
**Component:** Hydraulic hose assembly (main boom cylinder supply line)

**Condition:** ⚠️ Monitor

**Observations:**
- Hose shows minor surface weathering but no cracks
- Fitting at the cylinder end appears tight with no visible seepage
- Hose routing is clear of pinch points
- Minor abrasion mark at the midpoint where it contacts the frame bracket

**Recommendation:** Acceptable for operation. Add a hose guard at the frame contact point to prevent further abrasion. Re-check at next 250-hour service.

actions.name: start_inspection
actions.description: Begin a guided walkaround inspection with step-by-step checkpoints

example.output:

actions.name: inspect_checkpoint
actions.description: Submit a photo for a specific inspection checkpoint and get pass/fail assessment

example.output:
## Checkpoint: Hydraulic System

**Status:** ✅ PASS

**Observations:**
- All visible hoses in good condition, no bulging or cracks
- Fittings appear tight, no active leaks
- Hydraulic fluid reservoir is at proper level (visible in sight glass)
- Cylinder rods show normal chrome finish, no scoring

**Issues Found:** None

**Confidence:** High

actions.name: get_checklist
actions.description: View the full inspection checklist for a machine type

example.output:

1. Overall Machine Condition 🔴 critical

actions.name: identify_component
actions.description: Point camera at any component and get identification, function, and inspection guidance

example.output:
**Component:** Cutting Tooth (carbide-tipped)

**Function:** Primary cutting element mounted on the cutting wheel. Carbide tip performs the actual wood cutting while the steel body transfers cutting force.

**Inspection Points:**
- Check carbide tip for chips or missing pieces
- Verify tooth is seated fully in pocket
- Check retaining bolt torque
- Look for excessive wear on the steel body behind the carbide

**Common Failure:** Carbide tip fracture from striking rocks or metal objects in the stump area. Replace immediately if tip is missing.

actions.name: check_wear
actions.description: Assess wear condition of a component from a photo with condition rating

example.output:
**Wear Assessment: Digging Teeth**

**Condition:** 🔶 Service Soon

**Observations:**
- Teeth are approximately 60-70% worn
- Carbide tips still present but significantly reduced
- Two teeth on the left side show uneven wear (possible chain alignment issue)
- No broken or missing teeth observed

**Recommendation:**
- Plan tooth replacement within the next 20-30 operating hours
- Check chain tension and alignment — uneven wear suggests possible misalignment
- Inspect sprocket teeth for matching wear pattern
- Order replacement teeth: Vermeer Part# [from manual]

Serial plate and label OCR reading

demoConversation.content: I need to do a pre-operation walkaround on our S925TX trencher.

# 🔍 Pre-Operation Walkaround
**Machine:** Vermeer Trencher (S925TX)
**Date:** February 18, 2026 at 9:15 AM

## Inspection Checkpoints (7 items)

### 1. Overall Machine Condition 🔴
📸 **Stand back and take a photo of the entire machine from each side. Look for obvious damage, fluid puddles underneath, or anything out of place.**

### 2. Engine Compartment 🔴
📸 **Open the engine compartment and take a photo. Check fluid levels, belt condition, and look for leaks.**

### 3. Hydraulic System 🔴
📸 **Inspect the hydraulic hoses, fittings, and cylinders.**

...

📱 **How to proceed:** Point your camera at checkpoint #1 and take a photo. Say "Check the overall condition" to begin.
Here's a photo of the hydraulic hoses. [IMAGE ATTACHED] Do any of these look like they need replacing?

## Checkpoint: Hydraulic System

**Status:** 🔶 Service Soon

**Observations:**
- The main pressure hose (top) shows surface cracking consistent with UV exposure and age
- The return line (bottom) appears in good condition
- I can see a slight weep at the fitting where the hose connects to the control valve — this is early-stage seepage
- Hose routing looks correct with no sharp bends or chafe points

**Issues Found:**
1. 🔶 Main pressure hose surface cracking — plan replacement at next scheduled service
2. ⚠️ Minor seepage at control valve fitting — monitor daily, tighten fitting if accessible

**Recommendation:**
- Schedule hose replacement within the next 2 weeks / 50 operating hours
- Clean the weeping fitting and recheck after 8 hours of operation
- If seepage worsens to an active drip, shut down and replace immediately

**Confidence:** High — the cracking pattern and seepage are clearly visible in the image.
Can you read this serial plate for me? [IMAGE ATTACHED]

📋 **Serial Plate Reading:**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Manufacturer** | Vermeer Manufacturing Co. |
| **Model** | S925TX |
| **Serial Number** | 1VRH092RXP1002847 |
| **Year** | 2025 |
| **Gross Weight** | 9,250 lbs (4,196 kg) |
| **Engine** | Deutz TD2.9 L4 Tier 4 Final |
| **Net Power** | 74.3 HP (55.4 kW) |
| **Manufactured** | Pella, Iowa, USA |

**Decoded:** This is a 2025 production S925TX from Vermeer's Pella facility. The 'P' in the serial number indicates 2025 model year. Machine is within standard warranty period.
What are the digging teeth supposed to look like when they're new vs when they need replacing?

# Digging Teeth — Wear Guide (S925TX Trencher)

## New Condition ✅
- Full carbide tip height (typically 1.5-2 inches exposed)
- Sharp cutting edges with no rounding
- Tight fit in tooth pocket, no play
- Retaining bolt properly torqued

## Monitor ⚠️ (~50% wear)
- Carbide tip worn to approximately half original height
- Slight rounding of cutting edges
- Still effective but reduced cutting efficiency
- Plan for replacement parts ordering

## Service Soon 🔶 (~70% wear)
- Carbide tip significantly reduced
- Noticeable decrease in cutting speed
- May see uneven wear patterns
- Order replacement teeth now

## Replace Now 🔴 (>80% wear or damage)
- Carbide tip nearly flush with steel body
- Cracked, chipped, or missing carbide
- Excessive play in tooth pocket
- Steel body wearing against the rock

**📸 Send me a photo of your teeth and I'll tell you where they fall on this scale!**

**Tip:** Rotate or replace teeth in complete sets to maintain even cutting and chain balance.

Create a work order for the issues we found

technicalNotes.visionModel: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o (or GPT-4.1/GPT-5) with vision capability
technicalNotes.detailLevel: Uses 'high' detail mode for equipment inspection accuracy
technicalNotes.latency: Typically 3-8 seconds per image analysis depending on complexity
technicalNotes.costPerImage: ~$0.01-0.03 per image analysis (GPT-4o pricing, high detail mode)
technicalNotes.offlineCapability: Capture images offline, analyze when connectivity is restored
technicalNotes.enterpriseSecurity: All data stays within Azure tenant, no consumer API dependency
